agents.lastMessage: Introducing a bulky aromatic residue at position 5 and a negatively charged resi

report.hypothesis: Introducing a bulky aromatic residue at position 5 and a negatively charged residue at position 6 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6 while disrupting off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic complementarity, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis remains valid — strong SSTR2 binding is achievable, but s

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid — strong SSTR2 binding is achievable, but selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5 requires explicit disruption of conserved interactions. The next iteration should focus on tuning non-pharmacophore positions to break these interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 and improve SSTR2 selectivity, we should focus mutations at non-pharmacophore positions that are known to contribute to off-target binding.

Introducing charged or hydrophobic mutations at key positions can disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore region (positions 7-10) and disulfide bonds (Cys3/Cys14).
